## Summary

<!-- One or two sentences. What does this PR change and why? -->

## Linked issue

<!-- e.g. Closes #123, Part of #1 -->

## Type of change

- [ ] feat — new functionality
- [ ] fix — bug fix
- [ ] docs — documentation only
- [ ] chore — tooling, deps, or repo maintenance
- [ ] refactor — no behavior change
- [ ] test — adding or fixing tests
- [ ] perf — performance improvement
- [ ] ci / build — pipeline or build configuration

## Checklist

- [ ] PR title follows [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org)
- [ ] `make` (build + lint + test) passes locally
- [ ] New code is covered by tests; coverage for `core/...` stays above 80%
- [ ] Public APIs are documented
- [ ] Replay tests added for new examples (if applicable)
- [ ] No new dependencies introduced — or, if introduced, they are permissive-licensed and justified in the description
- [ ] No secrets, credentials, or large binaries committed

## Notes for the reviewer

<!-- Anything reviewers should pay particular attention to. Tradeoffs considered, alternatives rejected, follow-ups deferred. -->
